Official Educational Briefing

Federal Benefits
Masterclass:
Weber State University

A comprehensive briefing on FERS, TSP, FEGLI, and Social Security for Northern Utah Federal Employees.

Trust Signal Fiduciary Excellence
Track Record 500+ Feds Served
Event Type No-Cost Educational
Limited Seating

Secure Your Briefing

Complimentary admission for federal employees and spouses.

Privacy Protected & No-Obligation Educational Event

Curriculum

Mastering the Legacy of Your Service

"Your federal benefits are more than a paycheck; they are a multi-generational legacy that requires active stewardship."

CH. 01

CSRS & FERS Pension Systems

Understanding your pension calculation, survivor benefit options, and the strategic interplay between your pension, Social Security, and TSP in retirement planning.

Pension Logic Survivor Benefits
CH. 02

TSP & Social Security Optimization

Advanced Thrift Savings Plan strategies, withdrawal timing, and coordinating with Social Security benefits including the FERS Supplement for early retirees.

TSP Strategy FERS Supplement
CH. 03

FEGLI & FLTCIP Analysis

Federal life insurance optimization and long-term care planning. When to keep FEGLI vs. private alternatives and protecting against long-term care costs.

Life Insurance Long-Term Care
CH. 04

FEHB & Retirement Planning

Federal health benefits in retirement, Medicare coordination, and comprehensive retirement planning strategies that integrate all your federal benefits.

Health Benefits Medicare Timing
Training Authorization

Request Paid Time Off to Attend

Federal employees may be eligible to attend using an SF-182 Training Authorization Form if approved by their agency.

SF-182 Form

Federal employees may be eligible to attend through their agency's SF-182 training authorization process if approved as job-related. Use of an SF-182 is optional and not required to register or attend. Approval, when applicable, is determined solely by each agency.

Voice of Authority

"Host of Retire Right Radio, heard weekly on Northern Utah airwaves."

Your Briefing Officer

Mike Stevens,
CFP®, RICP®

Mike has dedicated over two decades to navigating the complex corridors of federal retirement. As a Certified Financial Planner™ and Retirement Income Certified Professional®, he brings a surgical level of precision to FERS and TSP analysis.

✓

Independent Fiduciary

✓

Host of Retire Right Radio on KSL & KNRS

✓

Specialized in Utah's federal corridor (Hill AFB / IRS)

Location & Logistics

Weber State University
Davis Campus

D2 Building, Room 117 (first floor, right inside main entrance)

Thursday, April 9th, 2026

9:00 AM - 11:00 AM

Covered by Capital Wealth — Print your parking permit (sent after registration)

Campus Maps & Navigation

Davis Campus Map Printable Campus PDF D2 Interior Map (Room 117)

Important Parking Information

Parking permits must be PRINTED and placed on the driver's side dashboard. Digital display on phone will NOT work. Valid in DAVIS W LOTS ONLY.

Training Details

Session Information

Room capacity: 25-50 seats
In-person session (not virtual)
Arrive 15 minutes early
Business casual dress code
Seats are limited — register early to guarantee your spot
Registration Information

What You Need to Know

✓

Attendance is free of charge

✓

Spouses/guests are encouraged to attend

✓

Training covers both CSRS & FERS audiences

✓

After the workshop, one-on-one help is available

Frequently Asked Questions

Is this affiliated with OPM?

No, this is an independent educational seminar. We provide non-biased, fiduciary guidance that is not limited by government handbook scripts.

Can I bring my spouse?

Absolutely. In fact, we encourage it. Retirement is a joint legacy decision, especially regarding FEHB and Survivor Benefits.

Will I be sold financial products?

This session is purely educational. No financial products will be offered or sold during the seminar. Our goal is clarity, not solicitation.

Register for the Briefing
